channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Noah Kahan’s ‘The Great Divide’: All 17 Tracks Ranked
item.description:
[Intro music fades out]
Host: Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest music news and trends. Today, we're talking about singer-songwriter Noah Kahan, who's been making waves in the music industry since the release of his career-shifting album "Stick Season" in 2022.
[Soft music plays in the background]
Host: "Stick Season" was a game-changer for Kahan, with a deluxe edition and a special edition with features from Post Malone, Hozier, and Gracie Abrams. The album's success even earned him a nomination for Best New Artist at the 2024 Grammys.
[Music transitions to a clip of "The Great Divide"]
Host: Fast forward to 2026, and Kahan is back with his fourth studio album, "The Great Divide". The title track became his highest-charting hit on the Billboard Hot 100, peaking at number 6, and scoring him his first chart-topper on the Hot Rock & Alternative Songs chart.
[Music returns to soft background music]
Host: Kahan co-produced the new album with longtime collaborator Gabe Simon and acclaimed songwriter/producer Aaron Dessner. The result is a sonic grandeur that sets "The Great Divide" apart from his previous work, with lush layers of instrumentation and refined vocal tricks.
[Clip of "Porch Light" plays]
Host: The album chronicles Kahan's experiences with success and how it's impacted his relationships, family, and home state of Vermont. With 17 tracks, the album offers a multidimensional perspective, making it a conversation with itself, and giving listeners multiple entry points.
[Music transitions to a clip of Kahan speaking]
Host: Kahan, known for his humor, took to social media to manage expectations ahead of the album's release, joking that it might just suck. But the anticipation was real, and fans were eager to hear the new album.
[Outro music starts playing]
Host: That's all for today's episode. Noah Kahan's "The Great Divide" is out now, and it's definitely worth a listen. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you in the next episode.
[Outro music continues to play until the end]
